channel.title: Firewall on Pi Stack
channel.description: Recent content in Firewall on Pi Stack

item.title: Self-Hosted DNS Firewall & RPZ Solutions: Unbound vs PowerDNS vs BIND 9 vs Knot Resolver 2026
item.description: A DNS firewall sits at the edge of your network, intercepting every DNS query and blocking requests to known-malicious domains before a connection is ever established. Unlike application-layer firewalls that inspect traffic after the handshake, DNS firewalls stop threats at the resolution stage — preventing malware C2 callbacks, phishing page loads, and cryptomining scripts from reaching your infrastructure.
